summary: Athletics Marketing Department

name: Tulane University Communications & Marketing
summary: Tulane’s Office of University Communications and Marketing provides strategies for news, media, print, web, video, photography, design and social media.

name: Tulane Family Programs
summary: The Office of Tulane Family Programs provides support, information, newsletters, webinars, and events for the families of undergraduate students at Tulane.

name: Department of Anthropology
summary: The mission of the Department of Anthropology is to provide undergraduate and graduate instruction in anthropology, and to produce innovative research and scholarship.

name: Carolyn Barber-Pierre Center for Intercultural Life - Tulane University
summary: The Carolyn Barber-Pierre Center for Intercultural Life fosters a welcoming environment for all students, with programs, services, and support that center on the experiences of historically underserved communities.

name: Tulane Institute for Physician Scientists
summary: The Tulane Institute for Physician Scientists (TIPS) is designed to foster and support the growing community of physician scientists at Tulane.

name: Tulane Athletics Department -Student Athlete Enhancement
summary: Tulane Athletics will hosts "Civic Engagement" programs for our student-athletes.

name: Tulane University Center for Continuing Education
summary: The Tulane University Center for Continuing Education (CCE)  is accredited by the ACCME® to provide AMA PRA Category 1 Credits™ and by IACET to offer general CEUs. CCE collaborates with departments or organizations to provide conference management.

name: Tulane Math Club
summary: Tulane Math Club is dedicated to developing collaborative mathematical thinking and providing students with opportunities to engage in the larger math community in New Orleans.

name: Epsilon Eta Sigma
summary: Epsilon Eta is the national co-ed professional fraternity for students seeking post-grad positions in the environmental field. Epsilon Eta Sigma serves that purpose on Tulane's campus through our three pillars: Professional, volunteerism, and social.

name: Big Easy EC
summary:
Big Easy EC is a group of dedicated volunteers committed to reproductive justice and
healthcare. Big Easy EC provides and delivers free and confidential emergency contraception,
pregnancy tests, and barrier methods to Tulane and Loyola students.

name: Tulane Center for Community-Engaged Artificial Intelligence
summary: Research Center of Excellence investigating new approaches to artificial intelligence that are inclusive, effective, fair, transparent, and accountable.

name: Enterprise Risk Services
summary: The Office of Enterprise Risk Services provides risk, insurance, and environmental expertise as well as emergency management and business continuity services through customized programs and innovative solutions.

name: University Compliance & Ethics
summary: Tulane's Compliance & Ethics Program supports and coordinates ethics and compliance functions across the University.

name: Department of Earth & Environmental Sciences
summary: The Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences at Tulane has existed under its current name since about 2003.  Prior to that, the department was known as the Department of Geology.  Geology was first studied at Tulane within the chemistry department.

name: Future Minds AI
summary: The goal of Future Minds AI is to keep students up to date on the latest AI trends via learning and working with AI. This goal will be achieved via listening to speakers on the topic of AI, hand-on projects, net working and many other avenues.

name: Education Research Alliance
summary: The Education Research Alliance for New Orleans (ERA-New Orleans) is a research center that collaborates with local education stakeholders to produce objective, rigorous, and useful research.

name: Tulane Human Research Protection Office
summary: The primary goal of the Tulane University Human Research Protection Office is to balance the protection of human subjects while promoting and facilitating the research enterprise, and to maintain an open and cooperative relationship within research.

name: University Financial Aid Office
summary: The University Financial Aid Office, a unit within Enrollment Management, continuously strives to successfully honor our mission “to assist students in financing educational opportunities through excellent customer service and professional teamwork.”

name: International Student Advisory Board
summary: The ISAB raises international student needs and concerns, participates in developing new initiatives, interacts with the wider University community to raise the visibility of international students, and develops international student leadership.

name: Phi Chi Theta
summary: Phi Chi Theta is a National Professional Fraternity in Business and Economics.

name: Tulane Minecraft Enthusiasts
summary: TME is a vibrant and inclusive community that thrives on the virtual landscapes of Minecraft, where students come together to build, explore, and connect in a digital world of limitless possibilities.

name: ReCoast
summary: Formed under the National Science Foundation Convergence Accelerator Program, ReCoast members are faculty, students, staff, alumni, and community collaborators working with Glass Half Full to recycle glass into sand for coastal restoration.

name: Sexual Violence Prevention and Response Collective
summary: SVPR is a collective of dedicated activists seeking to address and respond to rape culture at Tulane. We pursue initiatives that are both prevention and response facing to decrease the presence of sexual violence and support survivors.

name: Center for Inter-American Policy and Research
summary: CIPR is devoted to inter-hemispheric exchanges that will advance the production and dissemination of knowledge about critical policy issues facing the Americas as well as the deepening of academic research in these areas.

name: Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering Department
summary: The CBE's focus is to provide the highest quality programs to educate students at all levels in the principles and applications of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering, within the environment of Tulane University.

name: Girls Who Code - College Loop Tulane
summary: Girls Who Code Tulane fosters community within tech-related fields through events, guest speakers and help in professional advancement.

name: Tulane Turkish Students Association
summary: TulaneUTSA is an inclusive organization that brings together students of Turkish descent and those interested in Turkish culture and heritage. Our organization's mission is to promote Turkish culture on campus.

name: The Center for Public Service Events Portal
summary: The Center supports a University curriculum and research agenda by uniting academics and action, classroom and communities through which students, faculty, and community partners dedicate themselves to the transformation of civic life.

name: Law School Online Program
summary: Tulane University Law School offers master’s of jurisprudence degrees in Labor and Employment Law, Energy Law, and Environmental Law

name: Tulane Adult Infectious Diseases Fellowship Program- SOM
summary: Infectious Diseases Fellowship Program

name: Tulane U. School of Medicine Chief Strategy Office
summary: Tulane SOM Chief Strategy Office

name: Louisiana Center for College Access
summary: College and Career Fair

name: Tulane Law Library
summary: Tulane Law Library supports the research and curricular needs of law school faculty and students. Additionally, TLL welcomes the entire Tulane University during our public hours, including access to materials. Entrance: 3rd Floor of Weinmann Hall.

name: Management Communication Events and Promotions
summary: This organization will coordinate event scheduling for several MCOM 3010 Class projects involving on-campus business and non-profit promotion.

name: Tulane Association of Business Alumni
summary: While representing the more than 25,000 alumni of the A.B. Freeman School of Business, the mission of the Tulane Association of Business Alumni is to encourage active alumni involvement in support of the school’s mission and goals

name: Tulane Internal Medicine Residency Program
summary: Tulane School of Medicine's Internal Medicine Residency Program

name: Harm Education, Awareness, and Reduction at Tulane
summary: H.E.A.R.T. is a group of Tulane students committed to providing accessible, anonymous, non-judgemental, empirically based harm reduction and overdose prevention resources and support strategies to Tulane students and the broader uptown New Orleans area.

name: WaveWorks! Project
summary: The WaveWorks! is the two-year implementation of Oracle Cloud at Tulane.

name: Tulane Black Pre-Law Society
summary: The purpose of the Black Pre-Law Society is to empower minorities pursuing law careers by providing mentorship, resources, and networking opportunities, while also advocating for greater diversity within the legal field.

name: Karate Club
summary: Tulane University Karate Club will help teach students the art of Shotokan Karate. Sessions will be held under sensei's supervision, guiding students in learning, stances, strikes, and katas. Mental discipline will be emphasized as well in TUKC

name: Department of Pharmacology
summary: School of Medicine- Basic Science-

name: Tulane Rural Medicine Club
summary: This organization will focus on providing students with opportunities to learn about the various health issues facing rural Louisiana (ex: health care access, heart disease, diabetes, obesity, tobacco use).

name: The First TU College Krewe
summary: The First TU College Krewe was established to help foster a sense of community and support for First-Generation College Students at Tulane University through providing information, mentoring, and regularly scheduled events.

name: Feminism, Redefined
summary: A bipartisan discussion group committed to open-minded exploration of various topics relating to gender and sex including hookup culture, pornography, and how feminism affects men.

name: Freeman School Office of Graduate Programs
summary: With more than 25,000 graduates spanning the globe, our graduate programs are known for helping aspiring business leaders succeed in an ever-changing world. We offer more than a dozen programs—with full-time and part-time options.

name: Schwartz Family Center for Experiential Business Learning
summary: A center of excellence dedicated to high-impact, hands-on learning for students of the Freeman School of Business.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: The Tulane Chapter of the Society of Physics Students, also known as the physics club, is a professional and social organization for students interested in physics, regardless of major.

name: Data Analytics and Science Hub
summary: The Data Analytics and Science Hub (DASH) at Tulane University aims to foster a collaborative environment including students from diverse backgrounds: data science, medicine, business, and other fields.

name: Tulane Division of Enrollment Management
summary: The Division of Enrollment Management is a community of Tulanians dedicated to supporting students and families from high school through their graduation from Tulane.

name: Tulane Science Olympiad
summary: Tulane Science Olympiad is committed to hosting an annual invitational tournament for regional high school and middle school students to compete in a variety of science and engineering competitions to ignite their love for STEM.

name: Tulane Office of Emergency Preparedness & Response
summary: See Organization Description box

name: The Charrette Student Architecture Publication
summary: The Charrette is a student-run publication housed inside the Tulane School of Architecture. We showcase relevant student and faculty work spanning writing, graphics, and exhibitions as a means to engage with current dialogue at the school.

name: Value in Virtual Access Tulane
summary: Student-led initiative aimed at promoting digital literacy and making the most of Tulane's extensive technology resources. The club raises awareness and impact of cutting-edge software, cloud services, and digital perks provided by the university.

name: Alpha Alpha Alpha
summary: Alpha Alpha Alpha (Tri Alpha) is a national honor society dedicated to recognizing and celebrating the academic achievements of first-generation college students.

name: Leadership and Society of Human Resources Management at Tulane
summary: Bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world Leadership and HR practice by providing opportunity for leadership and professional development, networking, and career advancement.

name: Capital Projects
summary: Capital Projects manages design and construction of Tulane's renovation and new construction projects.

name: John Lewis Public Administration Program
summary: Are you interested in a career in public service?   Tulane SoPA's John Lewis Public Administration Program offers a Master of Public Administration (MPA) degree that prepares you to be a leader ready to tackle challenges and create thriving communities

name: Tulane Law Moot Court Program
summary: Moot Court Program for Tulane Law.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is a historically Black fraternity founded in 1911 at Howard University. Guided by the principles of manhood, scholarship, perseverance, and uplift, it promotes academic excellence and community service.

name: Office for Foundation Scholars
summary: The Office for Foundation Scholars supports students from cohort-based scholarship programs throughout their journey at Tulane.

name: Corporation and Foundations Relations
summary: We work with university leaders to identify and raise funding for school priorities. We assist Tulane faculty and staff in drawing private external funding for programs and research.

name: Stewart Center for Professional and Executive Education
summary: The Stewart Center for Professional and Executive Education is designed for working professionals seeking to elevate their careers without pausing their progress.  Stewart Center graduate programs combine flexibility with world-class education.

name: Freeman Marketing and Communications
summary: The Marketing and Communications team at Freeman promotes the Freeman School’s programs, events, and achievements through branding, digital strategy, and campus engagement.

name: Office of Student Conduct
summary: The Office of Student Conduct is here to facilitate your learning process in a variety of ways, including asserting the University's expectations of your time in this educational community. Expectations are encapsulated in the Code of Student Conduct.

name: Tulane Recovery Community
summary: The Tulane Recovery Community’s (TRC) mission is to support individualized recovery on campus by providing recovery-based programs that foster academic achievement, connection, and engagement.

name: SLA Deans Office
summary: Tenenbaum End-Of-Year Celebration

name: Tulane Rocketry
summary: We are an organization aimed at competing in aerospace competitions with other colleges throughout the country. We work with all majors with an interest in an aerospace career. We also do STEM outreach with the NOLA community.

name: Black Scholars' Circle
summary: Fostering a safe, affirming, and empowering community that centers the academic success, cultural identity, and collective growth of Black students at a predominantly white institution.

name: Deming Department of Medicine Section of Nephrology and Hypertension
summary: Section of Nephrology and Hypertension SoM

name: Colleges Against Cancer at Tulane University
summary: Colleges Against Cancer works with the American Cancer Society to host various interactive events such as Relay For Life to bring awareness and raise money for Cancer.

name: Tulane Baseball
summary: Tulane Green Wave Baseball Program

name: Youth For Innocence at Tulane University
summary: Tulane Youth for Innocence at Tulane University is a student-led chapter committed to raising awareness about wrongful convictions and helping exonerate innocent prisoners through research, advocacy, and community engagement.

name: Palestine Solidarity Coalition at Tulane University
summary: Through educational events, cultural programming, and advocacy initiatives, PSC Tulane will build awareness, empathy, and solidarity with the Palestinian people in their pursuit of justice, equality, and self-determination.

name: The Green Bandana Project at Tulane University
summary: The Green Bandana Project (TGBP) is a program for students that raises mental health awareness, reduces stigma, and connects students to mental health and suicide prevention resources.

name: Young Americans for Freedom at Tulane University
summary: A Reagan-inspired conservative group valuing intellectual inquiry and education through diverse speaker programming, student leadership development, and exploring traditional American values within Tulane's academic environment.

name: Tulane Energy Club
summary: The purpose of the Tulane Energy Club is to educate and inspire students interested in pursuing a career in the energy industry. Events include guest speakers, field trips, case competitions, networking opportunities, and a mentorship program.

name: Society of Asian Scientist and Engineers at Tulane University
summary: The Tulane Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers (TU SASE) is an inclusive STEM organization open to all students, focused on professional development, mentorship, networking, and community building at Tulane University.

name: Reality Television Fan Club
summary: A space for Tulanians to share their love of reality television on air!

name: AAPI Youth Mentorship Program
summary: The AAPI Youth Mentorship Program connects Tulane students with AAPI-identifying high school students, supporting them through challenges they may encounter on their path to college and empowering them to embrace and celebrate their cultural identities.

name: For You, Black Girl
summary: For you, Black Girl is a club that offers women of color a safe, welcoming environment to understand, connect, support, and network with peers and other community members, and learn of the experiences faced as a result of the identity that women of color

name: Phi Sigma Tau at Tulane University
summary: Phi Sigma Tau is the international honor society in philosophy. Its primary aim is to encourage interest and activity among students and to promote ties between philosophy departments. Tulane's chapter is the Louisiana Delta Chapter.

name: Tulane Global Health Alliance
summary: TUGHA is a student-run global health organization that transforms interest into impact through semester-long projects, research, partnerships, fundraising, and professional development, connecting students to real-world global health practice beyond camp

name: Office of Government and Community Relations
summary: Tulane University’s Office of Government and Community Relations builds strong relationships with government and community partners in support of the university’s teaching and research mission.

name: Freeman School of Business Department of Management
summary: Management Department of the Freeman School of Business

name: Texas Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
summary: Professional networking organization for law students interested in practicing in Texas.

name: Tulane Real Estate Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
summary: TRELS brings together students interested in real estate law and related fields, provides networking and professional development opportunities with practitioners, hosts educational events and panels on real estate topics, and helps members explore caree

name: Tulane Women in Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
summary: Tulane Women in Law (TWIL) is an organization committed to examining and discussing issues affecting women in both substantive areas of the law and in the legal profession. To reach this objective, Law Women sponsors speakers, panel discussions, and...
